blocks.text: Option One allows Flipcause to directly embed your campaign's widget onto a webpage.  The widget display will vary depending on the tools in use within your campaign.

Option Two utilizes a button that will redirect your supporter to your campaign widget. We can customize the button color within your Weebly theme, size, and descriptor using the themed buttons provided by Weebly for Webpack partners.
Campaign Widget Opens From a Button
Option Three will allow for Flipcause to create a custom button that will open the widget as a pop-up, on screen. We can customize the button color, size, descriptor, and more!

seo.title: Popup - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New
About - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text:
DOG.
From the underdogs of California’s overcrowded municipal shelters to the street dogs of Mexico, Family Dog Rescue gives dogs in need a second chance. We welcome these dogs into our San Francisco shelter and foster program, providing them with medical care, temperament analysis, spay/neuter, vaccinations and finally, a loving family of their own. Family Dog Rescue is proud to save dogs of all kinds, including puppies, adults, mixed breeds, purebreds, and dogs with disabilities.

HUMAN.
Our experienced, compassionate family of shelter volunteers and staff ensure that our dogs receive proper enrichment and socialization from the time they enter our program to the day they find their new home (and beyond). Our adoption process looks past any formulaic checklist of the perfect dog owner. We treat our potential adopters just as we treat our dogs – on an individual basis. We are proud to connect people to their perfect dog, creating families with every adoption.

FAMILY.
We believe that when you adopt a dog, a family is created. Through events and social media outreach, we’ve created a community-wide connection between Family Dog supporters, adopters, volunteers and canine alumni. Together, we advocate for greater public awareness about responsible dog ownership and celebrate the connection and love that rescue dogs so openly give.

​As a grassroots-grown, 501c3 dog rescue organization, we save over 800 dogs per year from certain euthanasia and place them with loving families. We sponsor Franz Ferdinand Rescue and the Blood Hound Gang, and have been visited or featured by The Discovery Channel, The Huffington Post, Martha Stewart Living, Uber, Instagram, Iam Somerhalder Foundation, and Chicken Soup for the Soul.
​

Angela was only 36 years old when she was diagnosed with a very aggressive, high-risk case of breast cancer, shortly after adopting her infant daughter, Bella.
“I will never forget the terror of hearing “You have breast cancer.” Time stood still.  My heart plunged into my stomach. I couldn’t breathe. I cried hot, angry, terrified tears.  I hugged Bella, my 6-month old daughter, as hard as I could.  I didn’t think I would ever be happy again,”
says Angela, reflecting on receiving her diagnosis.
As a corporate lawyer, however, Angela was never one to back away from a challenge.
“Time abruptly split in half: “Before Cancer” and then, “Fighting For My Life.” My motto became
Fight Like Hell
. I refused to give up hope. I was as fierce, kick-ass, and passionate about beating cancer as I was defending my law clients.”
Angela began volunteering at San Francisco’s public shelter, socializing and walking dogs there.  Eventually she became a foster to dogs in need (which, over the years, has totaled in the hundreds). She, like so many others, felt the incredibly healing power of the love these dogs gave her.  Angela always says, “Saving dogs helped save my life. The profound love that rescue dogs give to the people who save them helped me win my battle with breast cancer.”
​After a year-long fight, Angela was determined to be in remission. She returned to her job, adopted 2 more children and resolved to make the most of the rest of her life. She recognized the therapeutic role her rescue dogs, Hooch, Molly and Sparkle, played in her survival, and became committed to finding great homes for great dogs.
She wanted to open a physical shelter where people could come together to help dogs. A place where she could offer behavioral training and programs for kids and teenagers. And she did. In 2010, Angela founded Family Dog Rescue in San Francisco, California.
While the shelter has faced its fair share of challenges, just like our founder, we have never given up on our goal to save homeless dogs, no matter their breed, background, or disability.  We never back down from a challenge and, in doing so, have saved thousands of deserving dogs, and have become nationally recognized for our work with deaf, blind, and physical disabled dogs.
​

seo.title: OLD - Use of Forms in Flipcause - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text: It’s important to be aware of the different types of forms that are available to you within the Flipcause platform and for use on your website, whether it's a Flipcause managed website or otherwise. Click on each of the buttons below to learn more about each option:

​
Below is an example of a
Flipcause Volunteer Campaign
that has been customized to look and function like a fillable form or application. As you can see, the form can be embedded directly on a webpage of your choosing:

The benefit to using a Flipcause Campaign for collecting information is that all of the data automatically flows into the
Flipcause Activity Tracker
, and is available for you to be able to pull into reports as well.

This is an example of a form from
Mailchimp
, which is a
third-party platform
utilized for newsletters and email communication. The main difference with this form is that the data from it flows directly into Mailchimp, bypassing Flipcause completely. The code for the form is also generated by the software provider.
This is a great way to succinctly collect names and email addresses for newsletters and similar types of communications if you use a software such as Mailchimp, Constant Contact, Mailerlite, etc. We recommend you utilize a service like this if you are looking for a way to send mass emails and communications to your supporters, as Flipcause does not have that functionality. ​

This is an example of a standard Weebly contact form. All the data captured from these forms is contained within your Weebly website builder, although you can set up email notifications to alert you when the form is filled out. You can also manually pull the data into a CSV file.
​Regardless, it's important to keep in mind that data collected via Weebly forms
does not automatically flow into Flipcause or any other platform
.
**Only applicable for customers who utilize Flipcause managed website

seo.title: Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New - Blog
Integration Options - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text:
​All Campaigns and Campaign Menus that you create on Flipcause can be displayed in
two ways:
A Campaign Widget
A Campaign Landing Page
To learn more about the difference between a widget and a landing page, take a look at
this resource
.
The version of the campaign you’re planning to use (widget versus landing page) will determine what integration options you can utilize in the
Flipcause Integration Center
. These integration options provide different ways to incorporate your campaign onto your website.
Below we will outline the various options available, and provide examples for each.

A floating button appears on every page of your website in the same location at the edge of your browser. For a Campaign Widget, when you click the button it opens up a pop-up overlay window to your campaign.
Example Here
​

Note:
You can also test out the floating button x campaign widget functionality by clicking the "Support Us" button in the lower left-hand corner of this website page. Just keep in mind that the stacked and collapsible floating button featured here is only for Flipcause customers utilizing the Flipcause website package. This advanced code cannot be generated within the Integration Center, but you can still generate simple floating button code for use on third-party websites (as illustrated in the example above).

A standard button appears in a specific area on a single page of your website. For a Campaign Widget, when you click the standard button it opens up a pop-up overlay window to your campaign.
It's important to note that for the pop-up functionality to work, the button has to be generated in the Flipcause Integration Center, since your website's stock button won't have the pop-up function.

You can embed a campaign widget directly onto a specific part of a page on your website. This  creates a box on your website that displays the campaign directly within it, without any added steps to open it.
​
Floating Button x Campaign Landing Page

Similar to the widget floating button, a campaign landing page floating button appears on every page of your website in the same location at the edge of your browser. The only difference is that, when clicked, the button opens up your full campaign page in a new browser tab (or window).
Example Here

Note:
You can also test out the floating button x campaign landing page functionality by clicking the "Become A Sponsor" button in the lower left-hand corner of this website page. Just keep in mind that the stacked and collapsible floating button featured here is only for Flipcause customers utilizing the Flipcause website package. This advanced code cannot be generated within the Integration Center, but you can still generate simple floating button code for use on third-party websites (as illustrated in the example above).
Standard Button x Campaign Landing Page

A standard button appears in a specific area on a single page of your website. For a Campaign Landing Page, when the button is clicked, it opens up a new browder tab (or window) to your full campaign page.
Campaign Thumbnail x Campaign Landing Page

For a fancier version of the button above,  you can embed a Campaign thumbnail onto a specific part of a page on your website. Just like the other buttons for Landing Pages, when you click the thumbnail, it opens up a new browser tab (or window) to your full campaign page. This option is a great way to incorporate a visual when featuring your campaign on your website.

Outside of the six integration options outlined above, you can also obtain the
unique URL/link
to your campaign in either a widget or landing page format. All campaigns live on Flipcause servers, each with a unique link that goes directly to it.
You can use this URL just like any other, and link it to the navigation bar, an image, a button, or another element on your website. This is also what you'll use when sharing this campaign on social media.
You’ll notice your campaign widget's URL is unique and different from the landing page version.
​

blocks.text:
​Meet Lady! This beautiful black beauty came to us from Turlock. She is a four year old Kelpie or cattle dog mix.  Lady has perky ears and a wiggly body (some might say she needs a diet but we all have things to work on, right). Lady gets along well with other dogs at the shelter.  She would love a new family that can help her work on her leash skills and would prefer to live in a suburban or less dense area.  Lady is very treat motivated and would absolutely love to learn new tricks as she is very eager to please. Please come meet this beautiful Lady soon!
GENDER:
Female
SIZE:
60.0 lbs
PRIMARY BREED:
Australian Kelpie
​AGE:
5 year(s), 9 month(s)
COLOR
: ​Black and White

Lola is a true sweetheart! This precious three year old girl was dropped off at the Yolo County shelter as a stray after another dog attacked her. Here in San Francisco to start her next chapter, this girl has won over our hearts with her happy personality and fantastic manners.  She has been great with other big dogs in our shelter as well. Lola would do best in a home with no children and no small dogs.  She also loves to ride in the car! We think this great girl would make a fantastic first dog and a wonderful, grateful family member.  Please stop by and ask for Lola!
​
GENDER:
Female​
SIZE:
50.0 lbs
PRIMARY BREED:
Pit Bull Terrier
​AGE:
3 year(s), 9 month(s)
COLOR
: ​Brown and White

Lunabell is a sweet 2-3 year old Siberian Husky girl from Fresno. She was rescued off the kill list with her sister and brought straight to Family Dog Rescue where she is now happy and safe. She loves to give kisses and get lots of pets and hugs. Lunabell is a fantastic runner! She is a favorite to go for a run with. After the workout, she will enjoy cuddling on the couch. She can't wait to be a part of your family! **Please note: Lunabell is in foster and not at the shelter.  If you’d like to meet a dog currently in foster, please fill out an adoption application and once approved, we will set up a meeting by appointment.
GENDER:
Female
SIZE:
55.0 lbs
PRIMARY BREED:
Siberian Husky
​AGE:
2 year(s), 3 month(s)
COLOR
: ​White

Welcome the amazing Jack Russell Chihuahua mix Mr. Magoo! He is one of our Lucky Fourteen! This group of fourteen dogs came to us after their owner suddenly died.  Mr. Magoo is probably about thirteen years old, and with his age comes great kindness and wisdom.  He is a loving and gentle dog who loves to lay in the sun or nap in your lap.  Mr. Magoo loves to be outside and would make an amazing and grateful companion  to anyone who likes short walks and lounging at parks and restaurants. His dream would be to find a family or person who can love him for him and shower him with unconditional love.  Please, come meet this amazing dog! We think he will really enrich the life of a lucky family because of his unconditional love and gratitude!
​
GENDER:
Male​
SIZE:
17.0 lbs
PRIMARY BREED:
Chihuahua
​AGE:
13 year(s), 8 month(s)​
COLOR
: ​white, cream

Meet Winston! Winston was found wandering the streets of Mexico completely emaciated and paralyzed from the waist down. He was rescued and his wounds treated. He will always be paralyzed but he is still active. He loves to run around in his little wheel chair! He even loves to play with toys! Despite his hard past, he loves all people and other dogs. He would make a perfect little companion to a loving family. Stop by the shelter to meet this sweet boy!
​
GENDER:
Male​
SIZE:
12.0 lbs
PRIMARY BREED:
​AGE:
3 year(s), 6 month(s)
COLOR
: ​tan

Customers can choose to hide any of the icons below.
Customers need to apply the corresponding link to the corresponding icon using the settings.
Social Icons are placed in the footer of each site, unless instructed by the organization to do otherwise.

​The RSS Feed Reader in Weebly allows users to display data feeds from various external websites, including headlines, summaries, and links to full stories. However, it does not support displaying thumbnails or images alongside each headline/link. This limitation means that while users can aggregate content from other sites, the visual presentation is restricted to text only.
Customers who would like to use this feature should reach out to the Success Team.

Accepted image file types
are JPG, JPEG, PNG, and GIF. Customers should use high-quality, high-resolution images.
Images can be added as a:
Weebly does not provide a separate media library for images uploaded to the website.
Customers should use a unique name for each image.
Sliding Homepage Banner (requires Success Team assistance).
Horizontal Gallery (requires Success Team assistance).
Accepted video file types
are MOV, MP4, M4V, WMV
​
Note:
Flipcause cannot copyright any images on behalf of our customers, nor can we prevent them from being downloaded by others. Customers can consult the US Copyright Office or their legal team for more information on how to protect their images and/or content.

Gallery images are automatically resized to fit within the columns assigned.
By default three columns are assigned. This can be adjusted.

Campaign Landing Pages, Widgets, and Campaign Menus can be installed on a customer’s Weebly site.
The
Integration Center in their Flipcause dashboard
is where they can generate code to integrate their Flipcause campaigns.
For
floating button integration, the customer should contact the Success Team.
Campaign Integration Styles:
Landing Page:
A full-featured campaign.
Widget:
A more condensed version of a campaign, typically integrated as an embedded area or a pop-up activated by a button.
Campaign Menu:
Display a collection of campaigns using the campaign menu integration.
Campaign Landing Page Standard Button Example:
Standard button from FC Integration Center
Pop-up Campaign Widget from a fixed button via Integration Center
​Pop-up Campaign Widget from a fixed button via Themed Weebly button
Campaign Landing Page Campaign Thumbnail Example:
Open Campaign Landing Page in a new browser tab from Campaign Thumbnail via Integration Center. Thumbnails aren't available via Weebly
Direct Embed of a Widget is only available via the Flipcause Integration Center.

All of the options below are only available via the Flipcause Integration Center.
There are also floating button options for Campaign Menus. Customers should reach out to the Success Team for floating button installation.

Pop-up Campaign Widget Menu
from a fixed button
​Open Campaign Landing Page Menu in a new browser tab from a fixed button
​Direct embed Campaign Widget Menu on a webpage

Any third-party apps that have been applied to the site will display in the Installed Apps section.
Third-party apps
in the Weebly App Store may be subject to additional costs paid by the organization directly to the app developer.
Flipcause does not troubleshoot third-party apps.

seo.title: Alice's Adopt-a-thon - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text:
​Hello all!  Due to Covid-19 and social distancing restrictions, we will not be able to hold our regular in-person  Annual Adopt-a-thon.  However, we thought that was an excellent reason to get creative!
This year, our adopt-a-thon is going virtual... and musical!  Our dedicated group of volunteers will spend all day on July 15 creating short videos of our puppies grooving to music, ending with a bit of background about each puppy.  These videos will all be compiled and published to help pair these puppies with their perfect people.
​
To accomplish all this, we need your help.
We are seeking donations to cover: all costs and adoption fees (including veterinary procedures), the cost of hiring a professional videographer, and puppy transportation from the sanctuary to their new homes once they are adopted out.  Our goal is to make adopting these puppies completely free, to rehome as many as we can.  Our goal is to raise $3,000 by July 15.
We are also still seeking several volunteers for July 15 to help dance with the dogs!
Please click the button below to support our campaign by donating or donating your time (or both), and thank you!
​

We have three membership options available to serve individuals, families, and businesses in our community, starting at $5 a month.
We offer flexible options to pay for the year in advance, or in monthly installments.

Members-only events access

​

Members-only events access for all organizational members

seo.title: Events - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text:
WHEN:
June 1-30 @ 12:00 pm – 4:00 pm
​
WHERE:
Creativity Explored  3245 16th St., San Francisco, CA 94103
​
In celebration of Bitchin’ – An Art Show for Humans & Dogs, Creativity Explored is hosting Raise The Woof Adoption Days at the CE studio and gallery. Join us and meet dogs from Family Dog Rescue looking for forever homes. Enjoy the day with CE artists as you learn about the work being done at Family Dog Rescue to support senior shelter and rescue pets. If you’re looking for a new best friend, drop by and sniff around for loving dogs currently available for adoption.
​Stick around to check out Bitchin’ in the CE gallery! We will have free dog treats for canine art patrons and limited-edition products featuring dog-themed artwork from the show.
​​

seo.title: Volunteer - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text: How does a small organization like us save over 1,000 dogs a year?

We love having families and children volunteer at the shelter together. Children under the age of 18 must be accompanied by a parent, relative, or legal guardian over the age of 18. The guardian must be a trained volunteer or attend a Volunteer Orientation along with their child, as well as sign a release of liability form. Teens between the ages of 14-18 are eligible for our Summer Teen Internship Program as well, which allows them to intern over the summer without their legal guardians.

​Become a part of our Family Dog Rescue shelter family!
Volunteers help the shelter run by walking, grooming, playing with and training our dogs. They also help with shelter maintenance, administration, transportation and cleaning. By volunteering, you will learn more about how to train and care for dogs. All volunteers will need to attend an orientation and supervised walk, and we ask for a minimum weekly commitment over the first 4 months. All volunteers 17 and under should be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian.
​

Help staff our weekly mobile adoptions and special events! These amazing volunteers advocate for our dogs, help with transportation, set up and promotion. By meeting with potential adopters at our events, you will have a direct impact in helping our pups find new homes! Check out the adoption event calendar here! Email us today to find out how to become an Adoption Events Volunteer!​

Come in for a group volunteer day with your company!
Groups of 4-10 volunteers come in to help clean and organize the shelter, as well as play, walk and bathe the dogs! Shifts are usually on Wednesdays or Thursdays from 1-4pm. Sign up for a group volunteer day now.

seo.title: Donate - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text: ​Together, we can save over 1,000 dogs per year!
​Our donors help us take in medical cases, underdogs and lost causes, and help them find hope again.

Even the smallest amount help!
We couldn’t save over 800 dogs a year without you. Make a donation today!

Our monthly donors make sure we can keep our shelter open! By setting up a monthly, tax-deductible donation through the Leader of the Pack Program, you are helping Family Dog provide shelter, medical care, rehabilitation and enrichment for homeless, injured and neglected dogs every day.
Just $30 per month ($1 a day) makes a real difference in the lives of homeless dogs.​

Foster - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text: ​We couldn't save over 1,000 dogs a year without our dedicated, loving foster families. We bring the dog and supplies, you bring the love!

As a foster, you will play a vital role in helping our dogs find a loving home – from socializing, to bringing them to events, to sometimes just showing them what it is to be loved. Foster families must live in the Bay Area and be available to bring foster dogs to adoption appointments at our shelter in SF.

seo.title: Giving Tuesday - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New
Sam's Pupper Suppers - Safe Paws - Birdseye 2 New

blocks.text: Donate food to help local shelter dogs, and then stay to play with some very well-behaved pups up for adoption! Festivities begin at 3:00 pm on December 31st, and kibble will be served at 5:00 pm.

Drop off your unwanted, unused, or special bought pet food to help local shelters
​

Meet-and-greet, followed by feeding your new-found friend a special pupper supper
​
After-dinner playtime where you and your new best bud can get to know each other better

There are many different ways to get involved with Pupper Suppers. You can sponsor the event, volunteer to work at it, register to attend and reserve your spot, or simply show up, donate, and if you'd like, stick around to feed a dog and play!

Register to attend the event as a host for one-on-one dinner with a new pupper pal
​

Contribute to the event by collecting donations, walking/handling dogs, etc.
​
Promote Pupper Suppers by becoming a Sponsor! Your sponsorship will be essential in making this event happen.

If you don't live close by, can't make it to the event, or don't have dog food to donate, there are still options for you to help out! You can make a one-time donation yourself, and you can start a campaign to encourage others to donate as well!
​
